mentions.note: Up 14% in 2025, peaked at $555 in late July. Light Azure guidance in late Oct, raised capex growth guidance (negative). OpenAI 27% stake concerns now overblown. If Azure beats 35% guidance and hits 39-40%, stock roars. Cramer confident OpenAI funding will resolve. Holding for Investing Club. Down ~$60 from peak.
Down from $555 summer high due to AI capex concerns; rare entry point on quality mega-cap; playing with strong hand at this cost basis.
Data center energy costs pressuring stock; clean energy commitment hurting; needs cheaper fossil fuels or natural gas.
Down 6% YTD; high-quality tech being sold; consider trimming if overweighted.
Down almost 6% YTD as quality tech underperforms speculative names; fundamentals solid but rotation pressuring stock.
Magnificent 7 member; money temporarily flowing to storage plays but company has too much cash and smart leadership; patience needed.
Weak partly because all software under pressure from generative AI concern (false worry in MSFT case); shaved P/E multiple. Very difficult to own except select days.

Down 13% since October report after CapEx surprise reversal (now expects growth higher FY2026 vs FY2025). Need no new CapEx increases and Azure upside (39% vs 36% expected) plus Copilot/Windows enterprise clarity Wednesday.

Reports Wednesday. Watch CapEx guidance (no incremental increases desired) and Azure growth upside (39% vs. expected 36%). Copilot and Windows moat questions linger. Down 13% since last report; need reassurance on spending trajectory.
Beat Q4 on top/bottom line but capex +66% YoY on Azure AI investment worried Wall Street; stock sold off hard after-hours.
Scissors this quarter on weak Copilot adoption (1% of users) and insufficient AI capex, but smart leadership will fix it next quarter; waiting for velocity decline to dry up before re-buying.

Least magnificent of Mag 7. Convoluted conference call; unclear if management knew stock would get obliterated. OpenAI partnership status murky. Co-Pilot 15M paid users underwhelming. Windows forcing unwanted updates; sympathy fatigue.
Down 3%, already hammered; enterprise software provider caught in rotation despite enterprise integration; strong company but sector headwinds.

Azure deceleration (chips to Copilot); 15M Copilot paid users underwhelming; 45% bookings from OpenAI raises reliability concern; stock down ~10%, now 24x earnings—more enticing but needs better explanation.
Azure deceleration due to chip allocation to Copilot; weak Copilot adoption (15M paid users) disappointing; 45% bookings from OpenAI concerning; down 10%, now 24x earnings; needs better story.
Very worried about CAPEX spending on AI infrastructure; considering selling position despite Cramer's long trust ownership.
Azure hosts OpenAI; partnership with Nvidia for infrastructure; major beneficiary of CUDA ecosystem customer attraction.
Enterprise software giant under siege from OpenAI and Anthropic; investing in OpenAI (Frontier model distributed via Azure) but facing fundamental disruption risk.
One of greatest performers of all time; currently despised and unable to catch bid until rates fall; holding despite selloff.

Massive structural concern: dominance of Windows (captive corporate audience) + Copilot push everywhere, yet stock can't rise. Copilot seen as unusable; management oblivious. Cramer owns for trust but calls it 'on borrowed time' and 'bad job owning.' Existential Copilot problem.
Software empire under pressure from hardware/AI disruption; down 0.3% but worse mid-day. Taboo topic on Wall Street but real secular shift.
Jennifer's portfolio: Cramer swaps it out due to problems with stock trading and company execution; too close to SAP; not trading right.
Trading poorly; too similar to SAP; both have ongoing problems; Cramer recommends swapping out to preserve capital; software weakness broad-based.
Software textbook stock clobbered on AI displacement fears; rebounded 3.6% today but down from $555 peak; structurally weak vs. Amazon/OpenAI dynamics.
Software business growth slower than Cramer likes; at risk from Anthropic and OpenAI; but balance sheet strong enough to make major pivots if management acts.
Azure is core infrastructure layer for AI data center build-out; major beneficiary of enterprise AI adoption.
Azure cloud business robust; enterprise software performing well; Citi upgrade and reseller strength reverse bears; stock +6% today after months of derision.
Can surprise with spectacular data center numbers. Doing buybacks; need to find out details. Co-pilot demand remains weak.
Reports Wednesday; hyperscaler capex indicator. Watch capex plans and ROI commentary.
Big Four earnings tomorrow (Wed); serial underperformer in recent quarters; fell 10% day-of on Jan 28 2026; charitable trust owns; watch capex cautiously.
Software business underwhelming; Cramer didn't have much to say and prefers Ford Motor for dividend yield and value over MSFT at current levels.

18% revenue growth, 23% EPS growth, Azure +40% strong, but overall revenue guidance light, Copilot users 20M underwhelming, and $40B+ quarterly CapEx with 190B full-year forecast spooked market; weak narrative on how spending converts to returns; stock down 4%.
Appears stuck with legacy software out of favor; Azure strong but no independent cloud valuation as subsidiary. Stock feels left behind despite cloud strength.
Even Microsoft (software play) caught in market's software aversion. Cramer says market hates software even though Microsoft is core.
Stock benefited from DDOG-led software rotation (+), lifting index despite mired-in-low-400s trading. Cramer considering profit-taking after parabolic move but retaining core position.
Leading software rotation; up 3% today. Cramer skeptical of rally sustainability given prior pattern of torrential selling.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Enterprise software leader with expanding margins; subscription-model rate-proof; positioned for AI megatrend; survives recessions.
Historically software leader but only AI portion valuable now; non-AI software facing existential disruption from AI alternatives; traditional software losing pricing power.
Terrific cloud business offset by weak enterprise software and Wall Street disdain for Copilot. Cramer's trust owns it but warns: must fix issues soon or 'the stock will have to go.'
Cheapest software valuation but doing nothing while tech explodes; same EV/sales as Alphabet; Windows and non-AI software weak; will sell if next quarter weak.
Stunning strength as software sector recovers from recent weakness; magnificent performance after month of punishment suggests sector inflection point.
Working with Nvidia on RTX Spark Superchip development; strategic partnership validates cloud and AI positioning; gained 2% on news.
May need to issue equity to fund massive data center capex for AI infrastructure; existing shareholders face dilution risk if secondary offering announced.
May do a fundraiser per Cramer's speculation. Unknown if they'll need to raise capital for AI/infrastructure.
Equity dilution risk similar to Alphabet and Amazon if capital needs force secondary offerings before SpaceX IPO closes.
While Cramer likes Microsoft, he emphasizes the stock is far less attractive at $350 than at $250—valuation matters; higher prices make even great companies less desirable on risk-reward.
